description: Modular skill plugin architecture work queue for Clawd Studio AI designer agent

tasks.title: Define core skill type system
tasks.description: Create src/lib/skills/types.ts defining the SkillPlugin interface, SkillManifest type, SkillContext interface, AgentToolDefinition type, SkillHooks interface, SkillToolResult type, SkillEvent type, and the Discipline union type ('interior' | 'architectural' | 'landscape' | 'industrial'). These are the contracts that all skills must implement.
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/skills/types.ts exists and exports all required types
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
SkillPlugin interface has onLoad, executeTool, onUnload methods
SkillManifest has slug, name, version, disciplines, requiredSecrets, agentTools, hooks fields
SkillContext exposes projectId, userId, projectType, supabase, addFeedEntry, getEnabledSkills, emitEvent

tasks.title: Build SkillRegistry class
tasks.description: Create src/lib/skills/registry.ts implementing the SkillRegistry class. It must support: loadAll(enabledSlugs, context) to auto-discover and instantiate skills from src/skills/*/skill.json; register(skill) for manual registration; setEnabled(slug, enabled) for runtime toggling; getAllToolDefinitions() returning all AgentToolDefinition[] from enabled skills; executeTool(toolName, params, context) dispatching to the correct skill; fireHook(hookName, ...args) broadcasting lifecycle events to all enabled skills; getSkillsForDiscipline(discipline) filtering by discipline.
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/skills/registry.ts exists and exports SkillRegistry class
loadAll correctly discovers and instantiates skills from src/skills/*/skill.json
executeTool routes to the correct skill by matching toolName against manifest.agentTools[].name
fireHook calls the hook on all enabled skills that declare it in their manifest.hooks
setEnabled persists to project_skills table via supabase client
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
Unit tests pass (see task 1.8)

tasks.title: Refactor DesignerAgent to use SkillRegistry
tasks.description: Refactor src/lib/designerAgent.ts so that: (1) The constructor accepts an optional SkillRegistry parameter. (2) runFullOrchestration() no longer directly calls generate-render or mock-sourcing edge functions — instead it calls registry.executeTool() for all skill-provided capabilities. (3) After brief analysis, it calls registry.fireHook('onBriefAnalyzed', analysis, context). (4) After session completion, it calls registry.fireHook('onSessionComplete', session, result, context). Keep the fallback to mock behavior when no registry is provided (for backward compatibility during migration).
acceptance_criteria: DesignerAgent constructor accepts optional SkillRegistry
runFullOrchestration uses registry.executeTool for render generation and product sourcing
Direct calls to generate-render and mock-sourcing edge functions are removed from DesignerAgent
Fallback mock behavior works when registry is null/undefined
Existing workspace UI still works end-to-end after refactor
TypeScript compiles with zero errors

tasks.title: Add LLM-directed tool planning
tasks.description: Update the analyze-brief Supabase edge function (supabase/functions/analyze-brief/index.ts) to accept a toolDefinitions array in the request body. When toolDefinitions are provided, include them in the GPT-4o-mini system prompt as JSON Schema function definitions and ask the model to return a structured execution plan: an ordered array of { tool: string, params: object, rationale: string } steps. The DesignerAgent should then execute these steps in order via registry.executeTool(). This replaces the current hardcoded render+sourcing sequence.
acceptance_criteria: analyze-brief edge function accepts toolDefinitions in request body
When toolDefinitions provided, response includes executionPlan array
Each plan step has tool, params, and rationale fields
DesignerAgent executes plan steps in order via registry.executeTool
Integration test demonstrates LLM choosing correct tools for a given brief

tasks.title: Add project_skills database table
tasks.description: Create a new Supabase migration that adds a project_skills table with columns: id (uuid PK), project_id (uuid FK to projects), skill_slug (text), enabled (boolean default true), config (jsonb default '{}'), created_at (timestamptz). Add a UNIQUE constraint on (project_id, skill_slug). Enable RLS with a policy that allows users to manage skills for their own projects. Run the migration.
acceptance_criteria: Migration file exists in supabase/migrations/
project_skills table exists in the database
RLS is enabled with correct user-scoped policy
UNIQUE constraint on (project_id, skill_slug) works
TypeScript types in src/integrations/supabase/types.ts are regenerated to include project_skills

tasks.title: Build useSkillRegistry React hook
tasks.description: Create src/hooks/useSkillRegistry.ts. This hook accepts projectId and projectType. On mount, it queries project_skills for the project's enabled skills, instantiates the SkillRegistry, calls registry.loadAll(enabledSlugs, context), and returns { registry, enabledSlugs, toggleSkill, isLoading }. The toggleSkill function upserts to project_skills and calls registry.setEnabled(). Pass the registry into useDesignerAgent.
acceptance_criteria: src/hooks/useSkillRegistry.ts exists and exports useSkillRegistry
Hook loads enabled skills from project_skills on mount
toggleSkill updates DB and registry in sync
Registry is passed to useDesignerAgent
TypeScript compiles with zero errors

tasks.title: Rotate exposed Supabase credentials from git history
tasks.description: The Supabase anon key and service role key committed in an earlier commit (88c219b) are still readable in the public git history. Immediately: (1) Go to Supabase dashboard > Settings > API and rotate both the anon key and service role key. (2) Update the production environment variables with the new keys. (3) Use git filter-repo or BFG Repo Cleaner to remove the .env file from git history entirely. (4) Force-push the cleaned history. (5) Add .env to .gitignore if not already present.
acceptance_criteria: Old Supabase anon key is rotated and no longer valid
Old service role key is rotated and no longer valid
git log --all -- .env shows no .env file in any commit
.env is in .gitignore
Application still works with new keys
tasks.notes: Requires manual Supabase dashboard access to rotate credentials

tasks.title: Write unit tests for SkillRegistry
tasks.description: Add tests in src/test/skillRegistry.test.ts covering: (1) register() adds a skill to the registry; (2) setEnabled(slug, false) excludes skill from getAllToolDefinitions(); (3) executeTool() routes to the correct skill's executeTool method; (4) executeTool() returns an error result when no skill handles the tool name; (5) fireHook() calls the hook on all enabled skills that declare it; (6) getSkillsForDiscipline() filters correctly by discipline.
acceptance_criteria: src/test/skillRegistry.test.ts exists with at least 6 test cases
All tests pass with vitest run
Test coverage for SkillRegistry is >80%

tasks.title: Write integration test for DesignerAgent with mock skill
tasks.description: Add src/test/designerAgent.integration.test.ts. Create a MockRenderSkill that implements SkillPlugin and returns a fake render result. Register it in a SkillRegistry. Instantiate DesignerAgent with the registry and a test brief. Call runFullOrchestration() and assert that: (1) MockRenderSkill.executeTool was called with the correct tool name; (2) The result contains the mock render; (3) onBriefAnalyzed hook was fired; (4) onSessionComplete hook was fired.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test file exists and all assertions pass
Test uses a real SkillRegistry with a mock skill (no network calls)
Hook firing is verified

tasks.title: Add AGENTS.md and SOUL.md workspace config files
tasks.description: Create two OpenClaw-style workspace config files in the repo root: (1) AGENTS.md — defines the core agent's purpose, constraints, and non-negotiable rules (e.g. 'Always ask planning questions on first message', 'Never generate renders without a brief', 'Always cite product sources'). (2) SOUL.md — defines the agent's personality and tone (professional, precise, design-literate, uses correct architectural terminology). These files are read by the DesignerAgent on initialization and prepended to the system prompt.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md exists in repo root with agent constraints
SOUL.md exists in repo root with personality/tone guidance
DesignerAgent reads both files and includes them in the analyze-brief system prompt
Files are included in open-source documentation

tasks.title: Define skill.json manifest JSON Schema
tasks.description: Create src/lib/skills/manifest-schema.json as a JSON Schema document that validates skill.json manifest files. Include all required fields: slug (pattern: ^[a-z0-9-]+$), name, version (semver), description, disciplines (array of Discipline enum values), requiredSecrets (string array), requiredTables (string array), edgeFunctions (string array), uiComponents (string array), optional (boolean), defaultEnabled (boolean), agentTools (array of AgentToolDefinition objects with name, description, parameters). Also create a TypeScript type guard validateManifest(obj) that validates against this schema.
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/skills/manifest-schema.json exists and is valid JSON Schema
validateManifest() type guard exists and correctly rejects invalid manifests
All existing skill.json files (once created) pass validation

tasks.title: Build skill auto-discovery loader
tasks.description: Implement the loadAll() method in SkillRegistry to auto-discover skills. In a browser/Vite environment, use import.meta.glob('../../skills/*/skill.json') to discover all skill manifests. For each manifest: (1) validate it with validateManifest(); (2) dynamically import the skill's main TypeScript class (e.g. import('../../skills/render-gen/RenderGenSkill')); (3) instantiate the class; (4) call skill.onLoad(context) if the skill is in enabledSlugs; (5) register it. Log warnings for invalid manifests but do not throw.
acceptance_criteria: loadAll() discovers all skills in src/skills/*/skill.json
Invalid manifests are skipped with a console.warn
Skills not in enabledSlugs are registered but not loaded (onLoad not called)
Dynamic import works in Vite dev and production build

tasks.title: Build Skill Manager UI component
tasks.description: Create src/components/workspace/SkillManager.tsx. This component renders a panel listing all available skills (from registry.getAllSkills()). For each skill, show: name, description, version, discipline badges, enabled/disabled toggle, and a gear icon for config. The toggle calls toggleSkill(slug, !enabled) from useSkillRegistry. Add a 'Skills' tab to the ProjectWorkspace sidebar. Skills that are not compatible with the current project's discipline should be shown as grayed out and non-toggleable.
acceptance_criteria: SkillManager component renders all available skills
Toggle correctly enables/disables skills and persists to DB
Incompatible-discipline skills are grayed out
Skills tab appears in ProjectWorkspace sidebar
Component is responsive and matches existing design system

tasks.title: Implement per-skill config storage
tasks.description: Extend the SkillManager UI to show a config panel for skills that declare configSchema in their manifest. The config panel renders form fields based on the schema (text inputs, selects, toggles). On save, the config is stored in project_skills.config JSONB column. The SkillContext passed to skills should include the skill's config object so skills can read their configuration at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Skills can declare configSchema in skill.json
SkillManager renders config form for skills with configSchema
Config is saved to project_skills.config
SkillContext.config contains the skill's current config values

tasks.title: Implement skill lifecycle (onLoad/onUnload)
tasks.description: Ensure that when a skill is enabled via toggleSkill(), registry.setEnabled(slug, true) calls skill.onLoad(context) on the skill instance. When disabled, registry.setEnabled(slug, false) calls skill.onUnload(). In useSkillRegistry, add a cleanup effect that calls registry.unloadAll() when the component unmounts (project workspace closes). This ensures skills can properly initialize/cleanup resources (e.g. WebSocket connections, Three.js contexts).
acceptance_criteria: onLoad is called when skill is enabled
onUnload is called when skill is disabled
unloadAll is called on workspace unmount
Skills that throw in onLoad are disabled and logged, not crashing the app

tasks.title: Implement cross-skill event bus (SkillEventBus)
tasks.description: Create src/lib/skills/event-bus.ts implementing a SkillEventBus class. It should support: emit(event: SkillEvent) to broadcast an event to all registered listeners; on(eventType: string, handler: (event: SkillEvent) => void) to register a listener; off(eventType, handler) to remove a listener. The SkillRegistry should instantiate one SkillEventBus and pass it to all skills via SkillContext.emitEvent. Skills subscribe to events in their onLoad() method. Define the standard event types: FLOOR_PLAN_UPDATED, RENDER_GENERATED, PRODUCTS_FOUND, FURNITURE_PLACED, SESSION_COMPLETE.
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/skills/event-bus.ts exists and exports SkillEventBus
emit, on, off methods work correctly
Standard event types are defined as constants
SkillContext.emitEvent calls the bus
Skills can subscribe to events from other skills in onLoad()

tasks.title: Inject skill tool definitions into LLM system prompt
tasks.description: Update the DesignerAgent to pass all enabled skill tool definitions to the analyze-brief and generate-questions edge functions as part of the system prompt. Format them as OpenAI-compatible function definitions (name, description, parameters as JSON Schema). The LLM should be instructed to return a structured execution plan using these tools. This is the key integration that makes the agent skill-aware at the LLM reasoning level.
acceptance_criteria: Tool definitions from all enabled skills are included in the LLM system prompt
LLM returns a structured execution plan with tool names matching skill tool definitions
Agent executes the plan via registry.executeTool
Integration test demonstrates correct tool selection for a test brief

tasks.title: Add discipline-based skill allowlist
tasks.description: Extend SkillRegistry.loadAll() to filter skills by discipline. When loading skills for a project with projectType='landscape', only load skills whose manifest.disciplines includes 'landscape'. Skills that don't match the discipline should be registered but not loaded (onLoad not called) and shown as grayed out in SkillManager. This ensures landscape projects don't see IFC export (architectural-only) and architectural projects don't see planting zone tools.
acceptance_criteria: Skills with mismatched disciplines are not loaded
SkillManager shows mismatched skills as grayed out with discipline badge
Discipline filtering works for all four disciplines

tasks.title: Add skill hot-reload in development
tasks.description: In development mode (import.meta.env.DEV), use Vite's HMR API to watch for changes to src/skills/*/skill.json files. When a manifest changes, re-validate it and reload the skill (call onUnload then onLoad). This allows skill developers to iterate without restarting the dev server. Add a toast notification when a skill is hot-reloaded.
acceptance_criteria: Changing a skill.json in development triggers a hot reload
Toast notification appears on hot reload
Only works in development mode (no-op in production)

tasks.title: Write tests for skill discovery, lifecycle, event bus, and UI toggle
tasks.description: Add tests covering: (1) loadAll() discovers mock skills from a test directory; (2) onLoad is called for enabled skills, not for disabled; (3) onUnload is called on setEnabled(false); (4) SkillEventBus emit/on/off work correctly; (5) SkillManager toggle calls toggleSkill and updates DB; (6) Discipline filtering excludes wrong-discipline skills.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 test scenarios have passing tests
Tests use mock Supabase client (no real DB calls)
vitest run passes with zero failures

tasks.title: Extract render generation into render-gen skill
tasks.description: Create the src/skills/render-gen/ directory with: (1) skill.json manifest declaring the generate_render agent tool with style, view_type, floor_plan_url parameters; (2) RenderGenSkill.ts implementing SkillPlugin — executeTool('generate_render', params) calls the generate-render Supabase edge function and returns the result; (3) Add a renders DB table migration to src/skills/render-gen/supabase/migrations/001_renders_table.sql; (4) Move render display logic from AssetGallery into src/skills/render-gen/components/RenderGallery.tsx; (5) Write README.md. Remove the direct generate-render call from DesignerAgent.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/render-gen/ directory exists with all required files
Render generation works end-to-end via skill system
Renders are persisted to the renders table
DesignerAgent no longer directly calls generate-render edge function
README.md explains how to configure and use the skill

tasks.title: Extract 3D floor plan editor into floor-plan-3d skill and wire render pipeline
tasks.description: This is the most critical task. Create src/skills/floor-plan-3d/ with: (1) skill.json declaring tools: export_floor_plan_view, size_furniture, get_room_data; (2) FloorPlanSkill.ts implementing SkillPlugin; (3) Migration for floor_plan_data JSONB column on projects table; (4) In RoomEditor3D, add a 'Finalize Plan' button that: renders top-down orthographic screenshot via gl.domElement.toDataURL(), uploads PNG to Supabase Storage at project-assets/{userId}/{projectId}/floor-plan-top.png, persists wall/furniture JSON to floor_plan_data column, emits FLOOR_PLAN_UPDATED event with floorPlanUrl and roomData; (5) In render-gen skill, register onFloorPlanUpdated hook that stores floorPlanUrl in session context; (6) Update generate-render edge function to receive floor_plan_url and pass it as multimodal inlineData to Gemini; (7) Add spatial constraint text to render prompt (room dimensions, furniture positions, ceiling height); (8) Add size_furniture tool that updates Three.js scene and re-exports; (9) Add room label overlay (names + m² areas) to export image; (10) Persist furniture placements to floor_plan_data.furniture array; (11) Write README.md with pipeline diagram.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/floor-plan-3d/ exists with all required files
Finalize Plan button exports top-down PNG and uploads to Storage
floor_plan_data is persisted to DB
FLOOR_PLAN_UPDATED event is emitted and received by render-gen skill
generate-render edge function uses floor plan image as Gemini inlineData
Generated renders reflect the actual room geometry from the floor plan
size_furniture tool updates scene and triggers re-export
Room labels appear in exported floor plan image
Furniture placements persist across page reloads

tasks.title: Extract product sourcing into product-sourcing skill
tasks.description: Create src/skills/product-sourcing/ with: (1) skill.json declaring the search_products agent tool; (2) ProductSourcingSkill.ts wrapping the search-products Supabase RPC; (3) Move ProductPanel UI into src/skills/product-sourcing/components/; (4) Replace the mock product catalog with real data — integrate IKEA's unofficial API or use affiliate links from Wayfair/CB2/West Elm product feeds; (5) Write README.md. Remove direct mock-sourcing call from DesignerAgent.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/product-sourcing/ exists with all required files
Product search works via skill system
At least one real product data source is integrated (not mock data)
DesignerAgent no longer directly calls mock-sourcing
README.md explains data sources and how to add new ones

tasks.title: Extract IFC export into ifc-export skill
tasks.description: Create src/skills/ifc-export/ with: (1) skill.json declaring the export_ifc agent tool (disciplines: architectural, interior); (2) IfcExportSkill.ts wrapping src/lib/ifcUtils.ts; (3) Add export_ifc to the agent tool definitions so the LLM can trigger IFC export when the user asks for it; (4) Write README.md explaining IFC 2x3 format and BIM tool compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/ifc-export/ exists with all required files
LLM can trigger IFC export via export_ifc tool call
IFC file downloads correctly when tool is invoked
Skill is only available for architectural and interior disciplines

tasks.title: Extract auto-generate cron into auto-generate skill and fix security
tasks.description: Create src/skills/auto-generate/ with: (1) skill.json declaring the schedule_auto_generate agent tool; (2) AutoGenerateSkill.ts wrapping the agent-cron edge function; (3) Add CRON_SECRET environment variable check to agent-cron/index.ts — the function should verify a shared secret in the Authorization header before processing (currently it has no auth); (4) Write README.md.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/auto-generate/ exists with all required files
agent-cron edge function verifies CRON_SECRET before processing
Auto-generate works via skill system
README.md explains cron configuration

tasks.title: Build landscape-site skill (new feature)
tasks.description: Create src/skills/landscape-site/ as a new skill (disciplines: landscape only) with: (1) skill.json declaring tools: draw_site_boundary, add_planting_zone, generate_site_plan_render; (2) LandscapeSiteSkill.ts; (3) SVG-based site boundary polygon editor component (click to add vertices, drag to move, close polygon); (4) Planting zone overlay with color-coded zones (lawn, shrubs, trees, hardscape, water features); (5) Export site plan as PNG for render conditioning; (6) generate_site_plan_render tool that passes the site plan PNG to generate-render; (7) Write README.md.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/landscape-site/ exists with all required files
Site boundary polygon editor works (click to add vertices, close polygon)
Planting zones can be added with color coding
Site plan exports as PNG
generate_site_plan_render tool generates a render conditioned on the site plan
Skill only appears for landscape discipline projects

tasks.title: Extract core agent into @clawd/agent-core npm package
tasks.description: Create a packages/agent-core/ directory with its own package.json (name: @clawd/agent-core). Move src/lib/designerAgent.ts, src/lib/skills/types.ts, src/lib/skills/registry.ts, src/lib/skills/event-bus.ts, src/hooks/useDesignerAgent.ts, src/hooks/useSkillRegistry.ts into the package. Configure tsconfig for package export. Set up the monorepo with pnpm workspaces. The package should have zero Supabase-specific dependencies — accept a generic storage/DB adapter interface instead.
acceptance_criteria: packages/agent-core/ exists as a standalone npm package
pnpm workspaces monorepo is configured
Core package has no Supabase-specific imports
clawd-studio-gallery app imports from @clawd/agent-core
TypeScript builds successfully for the package

tasks.title: Write core package README.md
tasks.description: Write a comprehensive README.md for packages/agent-core/ covering: what it is (a modular AI designer agent for architectural, interior, and landscape design); quick start (install, initialize DesignerAgent, add a skill); architecture overview with a diagram; how to write a skill (link to SKILL_AUTHORING.md); configuration (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md); API reference for DesignerAgent, SkillRegistry, SkillPlugin interface; examples; license (MIT).
acceptance_criteria: README.md exists in packages/agent-core/
Quick start section works as written (tested)
Architecture diagram is included
All public API methods are documented
License is MIT

tasks.title: Write CONTRIBUTING.md
tasks.description: Write CONTRIBUTING.md in the repo root covering: how to set up the development environment; how to run tests; PR process and review criteria; code style (ESLint + Prettier config); how to write and submit a new skill; how to report security vulnerabilities (link to SECURITY.md); code of conduct.
acceptance_criteria: CONTRIBUTING.md exists in repo root
Development setup instructions work as written
Skill submission process is clearly explained

tasks.title: Write SKILL_AUTHORING.md
tasks.description: Write a step-by-step skill authoring guide covering: the skill directory structure; skill.json manifest fields (with all options explained); implementing the SkillPlugin interface (onLoad, executeTool, onUnload); defining agent tools (JSON Schema parameters); registering lifecycle hooks; using the event bus; adding a Supabase edge function; adding a React UI component; writing tests for your skill; submitting to the public skill registry.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL_AUTHORING.md exists in repo root
A developer with no prior Clawd Studio knowledge can follow it to create a working skill
All code examples are syntactically correct and tested

tasks.title: Create hello-world-skill example
tasks.description: Create src/skills/hello-world/ as a minimal example skill that demonstrates the full SkillPlugin interface with the least possible code. It should: declare one agent tool (say_hello) that returns a greeting; implement onLoad (logs 'Hello World skill loaded'); implement executeTool; implement onUnload; have a skill.json manifest; have a README.md. This is the reference implementation for skill authors.
acceptance_criteria: src/skills/hello-world/ exists with skill.json, HelloWorldSkill.ts, README.md
say_hello tool works when invoked by the agent
Code is minimal and well-commented
README.md references SKILL_AUTHORING.md

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with a CI pipeline that runs on every PR and push to main: (1) Install dependencies with pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; (2) Run TypeScript type check with tsc --noEmit; (3) Run ESLint; (4) Run vitest run for all tests; (5) Build the project with vite build. Add a status badge to the main README.md.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and runs on PR
All four CI steps pass on the main branch
CI badge appears in README.md
PRs with failing tests cannot be merged

tasks.title: Set up public skill registry (GitHub repo)
tasks.description: Create a new GitHub repository at clawd-studio/skills (or similar). Structure it as a registry with: a registry.json index file listing all published skills (slug, name, version, description, disciplines, author, repo URL, install URL); a skills/ directory with one subdirectory per skill containing the skill.json manifest and README.md; a SUBMIT.md guide for skill authors; GitHub Actions to validate submitted skill.json files against the manifest schema.
acceptance_criteria: Public GitHub repo exists with registry.json
At least the 5 built-in skills are listed in the registry
GitHub Actions validates skill.json on PR
SUBMIT.md explains how to submit a skill

tasks.title: Build clawd-skill CLI for skill installation
tasks.description: Create a packages/clawd-skill-cli/ npm package with a bin entry. The CLI should support: npx clawd-skill install <slug> — fetches the skill from the registry, downloads the skill directory into src/skills/<slug>/, and runs any required DB migrations; npx clawd-skill list — lists all available skills from the registry; npx clawd-skill search <query> — searches skills by name/description/discipline. Use the registry.json from the public GitHub repo as the data source.
acceptance_criteria: npx clawd-skill install render-gen works and adds the skill to src/skills/
npx clawd-skill list shows all registry skills
npx clawd-skill search interior returns relevant skills
CLI is published to npm as @clawd/skill-cli

tasks.title: Security audit of all skills
tasks.description: Conduct a security review of all skills covering: (1) Prompt injection — validate all user-provided strings before including in LLM prompts (strip markdown, limit length, sanitize special characters); (2) Tool parameter validation — add Zod schemas to validate all tool parameters in executeTool before processing; (3) Rate limiting — add per-user rate limiting to all edge functions (max 10 render calls per hour per user); (4) Storage path validation — ensure storage paths are user-scoped and cannot traverse to other users' files; (5) Skill manifest validation — ensure skill manifests cannot declare tools with names that clash with core tools.
acceptance_criteria: All tool parameters are validated with Zod schemas
Rate limiting is active on generate-render (10/hour/user)
Storage paths are validated to be user-scoped
Prompt injection test cases pass
No tool name clashes between skills and core

tasks.title: Write SECURITY.md and responsible disclosure policy
tasks.description: Write SECURITY.md covering: supported versions; how to report a vulnerability (private GitHub security advisory or security@clawd.studio email); response timeline (acknowledge within 48h, patch within 14 days for critical); known security considerations for skill authors (prompt injection risks, tool permission scoping, secret handling); reference to OpenClaw CVE-2026-25253 as a cautionary example of AI gateway vulnerabilities.
acceptance_criteria: SECURITY.md exists in repo root
Responsible disclosure process is clearly defined
Skill author security guidelines are included

tasks.title: Tag v1.0.0 release
tasks.description: Prepare and tag the v1.0.0 release: (1) Update CHANGELOG.md with all changes since initial commit; (2) Bump version in packages/agent-core/package.json to 1.0.0; (3) Run full CI to confirm all tests pass; (4) Create a GitHub release with release notes summarizing the skill plugin architecture, list of built-in skills, and getting started instructions; (5) Publish @clawd/agent-core to npm; (6) Announce on relevant communities.
acceptance_criteria: v1.0.0 git tag exists
GitHub release exists with release notes
@clawd/agent-core is published to npm
CHANGELOG.md is complete
All CI checks pass on the tagged commit
